item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am a benevolent commenter, but also not a very good critic.

This feels most like a very recent SCP which has, as one can see, only experienced one test. This feels as if it has a lot under the surface, but the Foundation hasn't scratched the surface of what the SCP really is.

Perhaps it would garnish more attention and appreciation if there was more successive investigation of the SCP. Why is it hostile if its room lacks objects to manipulate? How did we learn about that? How do we learn about its impossible object that was once a table? How best could we investigate the SCP? Observing it through infrared means would be a route to explore. How is "opaque" defined?

etcetera etcetera etcetera

anyways, this is a very old SCP and I am stuck in the era of writing SCPs for series IV. Maybe I should check out actually newer SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually it’s pretty good and for once I can think of more then one use for a being that is a mass of tentacles. 1+ vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh. Ok. I thought It can only reach into rooms with no people in it like how it cannot be in a room with organisms that have sight.

I will change my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They can see it's tentacles because it uses them to grab objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't very sense making.

How do people know it has octopus arms if they don't see it?

-1

I am not a meanie head.

item.title: Revision
item.content:encoded: Added Addenda, or whatever the correct plural is. Also trimmed the description a tiny bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the setup intriguing, although I agree with the above comments about needing further revision. I think you're on the right track with the last test but the main description needs a little more life. Giving it some specific behaviors could go a long way towards adding a bit of character. I was entertained by the idea though, and I don't think it needs to be much longer. It's almost all the way to being a pretty cool article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it does well explaining everything, but at the moment it just seems like a wacky monster that just sits around.
No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea was interesting, but the execution was bland. I feel like you were trying to imply something with the test, but I couldn't tell what it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the most interesting thing here was the experiment and description with the blind agent. All of the description about the mechanics of the being, and it interacting with doors and rooms and door cracks, didn't particularly have anything new or interesting going for it, and sometimes less than believable.

I feel like it would probably be better with less description and abilities. Find some core of it to focus on, and then expand that. Is it a wraith-like entity that has difficulty interacting with our matter/rooms/universe? Is it a petulant octopus being with cartoon powers? It's a bit all over the place.

-1, but only because I think it's worth another revision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is interesting. It's not bad by any means, and I like the details about what qualifies as a room, but it doesn't feel like there is enough here for an idea that has a fair amount of potential.

Novoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It can't enter a room with Windows. It says in the footnote it can only enter a room with opaque barriers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly doesn't do anything for me. Not good, not bad, just kind of gave a shrug at the end. Not sure what could be done better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But what if you herd it into a room with a window? You can still be in another room and look into the room its in without trying to enter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is my first official scip, tell me if it's garbage, tell me if it's not.
